fullNotes:
I. PRODUCTION & PROPAGATION
   - Sound is a longitudinal mechanical wave produced by vibrations.
   - It requires a medium (Solid, Liquid, or Gas) to travel. It CANNOT travel in a vacuum.

II. CHARACTERISTICS
   - Pitch: Depends on Frequency (High frequency = High pitch).
   - Loudness: Depends on Amplitude (High amplitude = Loud sound).

III. ECHO & SONAR
   - Echo: Reflection of sound. Minimum distance for an echo is ~17.2 meters.

questions.q: Through which medium does sound travel the fastest?
questions.explanation: Particles in solids are packed closely together, allowing vibrations to transfer much more quickly than in fluids.

questions.q: If you increase the frequency of a sound wave, what happens to the sound?
questions.explanation: Frequency is directly proportional to pitch. Think of a whistle (high frequency) vs. a bass drum (low frequency).
